info.title: SaasySquad API
info.description: SaasySquad Backend for Sengg2021. Uses a bun backend, neon postgres, and redis cache all deployed on Vercel :).

post.summary: Estimate market based on tags and category

400.description: Model not trained yet

500.description: Internal Server Error

post.summary: Retrain and save the model

500.description: Training failed

post.summary: Registers a user

requestBody.description: User details to be registered

user.description: User ID of the registered user

400.description: Email, password, and user not supplied or password is less than 7 characters

409.description: Registering email with someone that already exists

500.description: General error ocured during registration

post.summary: Logs in a user

requestBody.description: Login object needs a email and password

400.description: Email and password not supplied

500.description: General error ocurred during login

post.summary: Refreshes a refresh token

requestBody.description: Users refresh token to be exchanged

400.description: Refresh token was not provided

401.description: Refresh token does not exist, or token has been revoked, or token is invalid

post.summary: Logs out a user

requestBody.description: Refresh token to revoke when logging out

post.summary: Logs out all devices from a user

200.description: All devices have been logged out

post.summary: Sends a forgot password email to given email if valid

requestBody.description: Email to send forgot password to

404.description: Provided email not linked to any user

500.description: General error for failed email sending

post.summary: Resets the password of a user

requestBody.description: Email of the user, token which was sent to reset password, and new password

400.description: Missing token or password

404.description: Token has already expired or is invalid

delete.summary: Deletes expired refresh tokens stored in the database

post.summary: Adds an item to the user's saved items list
post.description: Validates that the item exists and appends its ID to the authenticated user's saved items array. Duplicate item IDs are ignored — adding an already-saved item is a no-op.

requestBody.description: Payload containing the ID of the item to save

itemId.description: The unique identifier of the item to save

200.description: Item successfully added to saved items

message.example: Item successfully added to saved items

400.description: Bad Request (Missing itemId in request body)

error.example: Need item ID in the body

404.description: Not Found (Item does not exist in the database)

500.description: General error for failing to add the item to saved items

message.example: Item failed to add to saved items

get.summary: Fetches the details of logged in user

500.description: General error to fail getting user details

delete.summary: Deletes the logged in users account

500.description: General failure to delete user error

patch.summary: Updates the details of logged in user

requestBody.description: Profile object: include at least one of email, password, username, or biography.

biography.example: Designer and collector of mid-century pieces.

400.description: No fields to update for the user

500.description: General error for failure to update profile

get.summary: Gets all active sessions of a user
Retrieves the details of a user given a user ID

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of user

500.description: General error for failed fetching of user details

get.summary: Retrieves all items for a specific user
get.description: Fetches a list of items associated with a given user ID.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the user whose items are being fetched

404.description: No items found for this user

500.description: General error for failed fetching of user items

message.example: Getting items by user id failed

get.summary: Retrieves saved item IDs for a specific user
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's saved item_id array from users.saved. The path userId must match the JWT subject; other callers receive 401.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user whose saved items are being retrieved

401.description: Unauthorized (Invalid or missing access token, or path userId does not match the authenticated user)

error.example: User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access saved items

404.description: Not Found (The user ID does not exist in the database)

500.description: Internal Server Error

get.summary: Retrieves the purchase history for a specific user
get.description: Fetches a list of orders where the specified user is the buyer. The authenticated user must match the user ID in the path.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user whose purchases are being retrieved

400.description: Bad Request (Invalid path components)

401.description: Unauthorized (User is not logged in or subject claim does not match the requested userId)

error.example: User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders
User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders
User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders

404.description: Not Found (The user ID does not exist in the database)

406.description: Not Acceptable (The Accept header requested an unsupported format)

500.description: Internal Server Error (Database or server issues)

get.summary: Retrieves the sales history for a specific user
get.description: Fetches a list of orders where the specified user is the seller. Requires authentication: the authenticated user may only request their own sales; the path userId must match the token subject.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user whose sales are requested

400.description: Bad Request (Invalid path components)

401.description: Unauthorized (User is not logged in or subject claim does not match the requested userId)

error.example: User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders
User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders
User is not logged on or lacks authorization to access orders

404.description: Not Found (The user ID does not exist in the database)

406.description: Not Acceptable (The Accept header requested an unsupported format)

500.description: Internal Server Error (Database or server issues)

get.summary: Retrieves the details of a specific item
get.description: Fetches a specific item from the system using its unique item ID, including its tags and reviews.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the item to retrieve

404.description: Item not found

500.description: General error for failed fetching of the item

delete.description: Removes an item from the system using its unique item ID.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the item to be deleted

response.description: Details of the deleted item or a confirmation object

500.description: General delete item failure

patch.summary: Updates the details of an item

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of item

requestBody.description: Item object that to contains fields that needs to be changed

500.description: General update item failure

get.summary: Retrieves a list of all items
get.description: Fetches all items currently available in the system.

404.description: No items found

500.description: General error for failed fetching of items

message.example: Getting all items failed to fetch

post.summary: Creates a new item
post.description: Validates the payload and creates a new item in the system, returning the newly created item details with a generated UUID.

requestBody.description: Item details required for creation

400.description: Bad Request due to invalid or missing fields

415.description: Unsupported Media Type (missing or invalid Content-Type)

message.example: This content type is not supported

post.summary: Validates an order payload
post.description: Checks if the provided order data meets all structural and type requirements, including nested order lines.

requestBody.description: The order data payload to be validated

orderLines.description: List of items in the order

415.description: Unsupported Media Type (Missing or invalid Content-Type)

message.example: This content type is not supported

422.description: Validation Failed (Missing fields, wrong types, or invalid order lines)

message.example: The request body is missing mandatory fields

post.summary: Create a new order and generate UBL XML
post.description: Creates a new order between a buyer (determined by the auth token) and a seller. Validates all required billing and currency fields, as well as the items in the order lines. On success, this endpoint returns an XML string (UBL format) instead of JSON.

requestBody.description: The order payload containing currency, billing, and line item details.

sellerId.description: UUID of the seller. Cannot be the same as the authenticated buyer.

orderLines.description: Must be a non-empty array of items being purchased

200.description: Order successfully created. Returns the raw UBL XML document.

400.description: Bad Request - The buyer and seller IDs are the same

error.example: sellerId and buyerId must be unique
sellerId and buyerId must be unique

415.description: Unsupported Media Type - The Content-Type header is not application/json

message.example: This content type is not supported
This content type is not supported

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - Missing mandatory fields, incorrect data types, or invalid order lines

message.example: The request body is missing mandatory fields
The request body is missing mandatory fields

500.description: Internal Server Error - Order creation or XML generation failed

get.description: Fetches the details of a specific order using its unique order ID. Returns the full order JSON object.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the order to retrieve

400.description: Bad Request (Order ID is syntactically invalid or exceeds 100 characters)

message.example: The id provided is syntactically invalid

404.description: Not Found (The order does not exist in the database)

message.example: Id does not exist or is invalid

500.description: Internal Server Error (Unexpected database or server issues)

message.example: An internal error occured while executing the operation

put.description: Updates an order based on the provided order ID and update payload. The user making the request must be the buyer of the order.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the order to update

requestBody.description: Payload containing the user ID and the updates to be applied

userId.description: The ID of the user requesting the update (must match the buyerId of the order)

updates.description: The fields to update on the order

400.description: Bad Request (Missing orderId in path)

403.description: Forbidden (User is not the buyer of the order)

404.description: Not Found (Order does not exist)

delete.description: Deletes an order based on the provided order ID. The user making the request must be the original buyer of the order.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the order to delete

400.description: Bad Request (Missing or invalid orderId)

403.description: Forbidden (User is not the buyer of the order)

message.example: User does not have permission to delete order.

404.description: Not Found (Order does not exist in the database)

post.summary: Adds an item to the user's shopping cart
post.description: Validates item availability and adds the specified quantity of an item to the user's cart in Redis. The cart expires after 24 hours.

requestBody.description: Payload containing the item ID and the quantity to add to the cart

itemId.description: The unique identifier of the item

quantity.description: The number of items to add to the cart

400.description: Bad Request (Missing fields or insufficient stock)

404.description: Not Found (Item does not exist in the database)

500.description: General error for failing to add the item to the cart

message.example: Item failed to add to cart

delete.summary: Removes a specific item from the cart
delete.description: Deletes a specific item from the authenticated user's shopping cart using its item ID.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the item to remove from the cart

200.description: Item successfully removed from cart, or it was not in the cart

500.description: General error failing to remove the item from Redis

message.example: Item/s failed to remove from cart

patch.summary: Updates the quantity of an item in the cart
patch.description: Updates the quantity of a specific item in the user's cart. Validates that the requested quantity is greater than zero and does not exceed available stock.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the item in the cart to update

requestBody.description: Payload containing the new quantity for the item

quantity.description: The new quantity for the item (must be greater than 0)

200.description: Item successfully updated in the cart

400.description: Bad Request (Missing quantity, zero/negative quantity, or requested quantity exceeds stock)

404.description: Not Found (Item does not exist in the database)

500.description: General error for failing to update the cart in Redis

get.description: Fetches the current user's cart from Redis, joins it with item details from the PostgreSQL database, and calculates the total cost. Note: This route requires authentication.

200.description: Cart successfully retrieved. Returns an empty items array and a subtotal of 0 if the cart is empty.

items.description: List of items currently in the cart

quantity.description: Quantity of this item in the cart (fetched from Redis)

itemTotal.description: Calculated total for this specific item (price * quantity)

subtotal.description: The calculated total cost of all items in the cart

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to fetch cart from Redis or database

delete.description: Removes all items from the authenticated user's shopping cart.

200.description: Cart successfully cleared, or it was already empty

500.description: General error failing to clear the cart in Redis

message.example: Item/s failed to remove from cart

post.summary: Creates a Stripe checkout session
post.description: Retrieves the user's cart from Redis, validates the items against the database, and creates an embedded Stripe checkout session for payment processing.

requestBody.description: Payload containing the seller ID and the buyer's email for the checkout session

sellerId.description: The unique identifier of the seller

email.description: The email address of the buyer for the checkout session

200.description: Checkout session successfully created or an early return message

clientSecret.description: The Stripe client secret required to initialize the embedded checkout on the frontend

message.description: Message returned if the cart is empty or no items are found

400.description: Bad Request (Missing email or sellerId)

500.description: General server error or Stripe API failure

get.summary: Retrieves the status of a Stripe checkout session
get.description: Fetches the current status and customer email of a specific Stripe checkout session using its session ID provided in the URL path.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Stripe checkout session

status.description: The current status of the Stripe checkout session (e.g., 'complete', 'open', 'expired')

customer_email.description: The email address provided by the customer during checkout

400.description: Bad Request (Session ID is missing from the URL path)

message.example: Session ID is missing from the request path

500.description: Internal Server Error (Failed to retrieve session from Stripe)

post.summary: Handles Stripe webhook events
post.description: Receives asynchronous event notifications from Stripe (e.g., checkout.session.completed). The payload is verified using the stripe-signature header. If verified and the event type matches, it fulfills the checkout.

parameters.name: stripe-signature
parameters.description: The signature sent by Stripe to verify the webhook payload

requestBody.description: The raw Stripe Event object payload

schema.description: See Stripe API documentation for the Event object structure

200.description: Webhook received, verified, and checkout successfully fulfilled

400.description: Bad Request (Missing body, missing signature, invalid signature, or invalid event type during fulfillment)

message.description: Provided if the webhook construction fails

404.description: Not Found (The event type received is not handled by this webhook)

post.summary: Retrieves a market price estimate based on tags and category
post.description: Forwards item tags and category to the external prediction service to generate an optimal selling price.

requestBody.description: Details required for price estimation

prediction.description: The prediction data returned from the external Railway service

400.description: Tags or category not provided

500.description: Internal server error or upstream prediction service failure

error.description: Error details from the server or upstream Railway service

post.summary: Generate AI-based item recommendations from an image
post.description: Analyzes a base64-encoded image using the Gemini API to identify aesthetic tags, then returns matching items from the specified category. **WARNING: This endpoint consumes external AI tokens. Please implement strict rate-limiting and debounce inputs on the client side. Avoid excessive or automated polling during testing to prevent exhausting API quotas.**

requestBody.description: Image data and item category for AI analysis

image.description: Base64 encoded image string including the data URI scheme (must be JPEG, PNG, or WEBP).

200.description: Recommendations generated successfully. Note: The 'items' array may be omitted if no database matches are found for the generated tags.

message.description: An AI-generated explanation of the chosen aesthetic, or alternative suggestions if no exact matches are found.
message.example: I chose these tags because the room features warm wood tones and clean minimalist lines.

items.description: List of recommended items matching the AI-generated tags and selected category.

400.description: Bad Request - Missing parameters or invalid image format

message.example: Invalid image format. Please upload a JPEG, PNG, or WEBP.

404.description: Not Found - No aesthetic tags exist in the database to match against

500.description: Internal Server Error - AI prompt failed or database query error

post.summary: Adds tags to a specific item
post.description: Associates a list of aesthetic or descriptive tags with a specific item. The authenticated user must be the verified seller of the item to perform this action.

requestBody.description: Item ID and the array of tags to add

itemId.description: The unique identifier of the item

tags.description: A list of tag strings to associate with the item

200.description: Tags successfully added to the item

400.description: Bad Request - Missing itemId or tags array is empty

401.description: Unauthorized - Authenticated user is not the seller of the item

message.example: User does not own the item

404.description: Not Found - The specified item does not exist

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database query failed

delete.summary: Removes tags from a specific item
delete.description: Removes a specified list of tags from an item. The tags should be provided as a comma-separated list in the query string.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the item

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of tags to remove (e.g., ?tags=minimalist,leather)

200.description: Tags successfully removed from the item

400.description: Bad Request - Missing itemId, missing tags query parameter, or empty tags list

message.example: No itemId or tags are not provided

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database query failed

get.summary: Get all reviews for an item
get.description: Fetches all reviews left on a specific item, ordered from newest to oldest.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the item whose reviews to retrieve

200.description: Reviews successfully fetched (array may be empty)

404.description: Item not found

500.description: Internal Server Error

get.summary: Initiates the Google OAuth2 login flow
get.description: Generates OAuth state and PKCE code verifier, sets them as secure HttpOnly cookies, and redirects the user to the Google consent screen.

302.description: Redirects the browser to the Google Authorization URL.

get.description: Handles the redirect back from Google. Validates the authorization code and state against stored cookies, fetches the Google user profile, and either registers or logs in the user. Finally, it redirects to the frontend application.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The authorization code returned by Google

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state parameter returned by Google to prevent CSRF attacks

302.description: Redirects to the frontend. On success, tokens are appended as a URL fragment (e.g., `/auth/success#access_token=...&refresh_token=...`). On failure, redirects to the login page with an error query parameter (e.g., `/login?error=google_auth_failed`).

post.summary: Sends a password reset email (v2)
post.description: Generates a unique reset token and dispatches an email containing the secure recovery link. Note: This endpoint confirms whether a user exists, which may allow for email enumeration.

requestBody.description: User email required to dispatch the reset link

200.description: Email containing the reset token was successfully dispatched

400.description: Bad Request - Email address was not provided in the payload

404.description: Not Found - The provided email does not exist in the database

500.description: Internal Server Error - SMTP transport failed to send the email

get.description: Fetches a complete list of all available item categories from the database. Note: This route is protected by the `authHelper` middleware and requires a valid user session.

items.description: Category details (e.g., category ID, name, description)

404.description: Not Found - No categories currently exist in the database

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database query failed

post.summary: Creates a new item (v2)
post.description: Creates a new item listing under the authenticated user's account. This v2 endpoint accepts a unified payload including category and tags.

requestBody.description: Item details including pricing, inventory, category, and tags

description.description: Optional detailed description of the item
description.example: A beautifully restored mid-century leather sofa.

imageUrl.description: Optional URL pointing to the item's image

tags.description: Must contain at least one tag

item.description: The created item record returned from the database

400.description: Bad Request - Missing required fields, invalid data types, or negative numerical values

415.description: Unsupported Media Type - The Content-Type header is missing or not application/json

message.example: This content type is not supported

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database insertion failed

post.summary: Verifies a Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) code
post.description: Validates a TOTP code provided by the user against their stored secret. Upon successful validation, fully enables 2FA for the authenticated user's account.

requestBody.description: The 6-digit TOTP code generated by the user's authenticator app

200.description: 2FA code was successfully verified and enabled on the account

400.description: Bad Request - Missing code, 2FA not initialized, or invalid code

500.description: Internal Server Error - Verification or database update failed

post.summary: Generates a 2FA secret and QR code for setup
post.description: Initializes Two-Factor Authentication for the authenticated user by generating a new TOTP secret. Returns a base64-encoded QR code that the user can scan with an authenticator app (like Google Authenticator or Authy).

200.description: 2FA secret generated and QR code successfully created

qrCode.description: A base64-encoded data URI of the QR code image.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to generate secret, update database, or create QR code

post.summary: Applies a discount voucher to the user's session
post.description: Validates a voucher code against the database for existence, expiry, and usage limits. If valid, the voucher is cached in Redis for the current user session (1 hour) to be applied during the final checkout calculation.

requestBody.description: The alphanumeric voucher code to be applied.

200.description: Voucher successfully validated and applied to the session.

discount_percent.description: The percentage discount provided by the voucher.

400.description: Bad Request - Missing code, voucher expired, or usage limit reached.

404.description: Not Found - The provided voucher code does not exist.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Redis or Database connection failure.

get.summary: Check API and database health status
get.description: Verifies that the backend service is running and can successfully connect to the database. This endpoint is public and does not require authentication.

500.description: Internal Server Error (Unexpected code execution failure)

message.example: Unexpected error that prevented request to be fulfiled
Temporarily lost connection to the database
